title: Amber Residence
scope: Full interior design · Sourcing & styling · Supervision
summary: Warm wood panelling and sculptural seating shape a family home of quiet luxury.
body: PLACEHOLDER PROJECT — A family of five asked for a home that felt calm without feeling empty. We answered with walnut panelling, boucle seating in warm amber, and light that moves across the rooms through the day.
Every piece was sourced against a strict material story: nothing shiny, everything tactile. The result is a home that photographs quietly and lives generously.

title: Sandline Loft
summary: Minimalist surfaces meet natural light in this contemporary urban sanctuary.
body: PLACEHOLDER PROJECT — An open-plan loft with generous glazing called for restraint: sand-toned plaster, concrete underfoot, champagne metal details.
We resolved the floor plan before a single purchase — zoning living, work and rest with furniture rather than walls.

title: Cream Court
summary: Neutral tones and layered textures create a serene backdrop for refined living.
body: PLACEHOLDER PROJECT — Cream on cream only works when texture does the talking: linen drapery, plastered walls, a boucle sofa that catches the afternoon sun.
Soft gold hardware and ceramics were the only permitted shine.

title: Haven Suite
summary: Bold geometry and warm materials craft a deeply personal retreat.
body: PLACEHOLDER PROJECT — A principal suite rebuilt from the studs: olive joinery, brass reading lights, an ochre throw that ties the palette to the client's art collection.
The fit-out ran six weeks, supervised daily.

title: Terra Lounge
summary: Earth tones and natural elements forge a timeless environment for hospitality.
body: PLACEHOLDER PROJECT — A hospitality lounge where guests should want to stay one drink longer: terracotta seating, charcoal stone, sage accents under warm dimmed light.
Durability specifications met residential softness — commercial interiors don't have to feel commercial.

title: Atrium House
scope: Full interior design · Space planning · Styling
summary: Soaring volumes and natural materials celebrate the art of light and space.
body: PLACEHOLDER PROJECT — A double-height atrium demanded humility from everything beneath it: low oak furniture, ivory textiles, and sightlines kept deliberately clear.
Styling finished the story — sculptural ceramics and a single oversized pendant scaled to the volume.
